118591.explanation: When forming the council, we find it more important to have the best administrators.

118592.explanation: In exceptional cases, a consultative referendum can be organized to ask residents for their opinion.

118596.explanation: This does not fall within the core tasks and limited financial means of the municipality of Lopik. Moreover, there are national subsidies for this.

118597.explanation: Each individual request from an initiator or entrepreneur must be assessed against the applicable policy.

118603.explanation: We support an initiative for this, but organizing it is not a municipal task.

118608.explanation: Within our minimum income schemes, we must ensure that especially children are not victims of the financial situation they grow up in. Everyone should be able to participate fully in society.

118610.explanation: Not all school buildings need improvement. However, we must take steps for new construction of outdated schools.

118611.explanation: Yes, for children from low-income families. Within our minimum income schemes, we must ensure that especially children do not suffer from the financial situations they grow up in. Everyone should be able to participate fully in society.

118612.explanation: Depending on the social importance and required resources, the municipality of Lopik must weigh whether to proceed or not.

118615.explanation: Lopik is a safe municipality, but with current capacity we cannot always deploy community officers everywhere; we would like this to change.

118620.explanation: We strive for a full-fledged home for everyone.

118622.explanation: Social assistance is intended as a social safety net. Not everyone on social assistance is able to work (e.g. due to illness, caregiving duties, psychological problems). A general obligation does not sufficiently take this into account. However, we do want to encourage participation in society.

118626.explanation: Based on demand, there must be sufficient charging points.

118628.explanation: Freedom of religion and belief is a fundamental right. This includes the ability to choose an appropriate care provider.

118630.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is an important source of income for the municipality. Reduction means less money for facilities such as road maintenance, greenery management, and services. Less income also means less funding for youth work, care, sports, and culture, and mainly affects the vulnerable in our municipality.

118633.explanation: Our preference is to expand existing business parks; there is demand among local entrepreneurs for this, but not for randomly planning new business parks.

118634.explanation: Sunday is the day of rest.

118637.explanation: There are sufficient other options for solar panels, such as on the roofs of homes, businesses, and public buildings. These options must be fully utilized first.

118641.explanation: Social (rental) housing ensures that people with low or middle incomes can live affordably. This is essential in current times of rising house prices.

118642.explanation: The current village halls must remain; a new shopping center is not appropriate everywhere.

118644.explanation: This fits within our vision and policy regarding accelerating affordable housing construction.

118647.explanation: The municipality must actively participate in the renewal of the current wind turbine park and the proceeds should benefit the entire community.

141775.explanation: Distributing asylum seekers (other than recognized refugees) across the districts is not feasible. We lack the money, personnel, and housing to do this properly. We also receive no government support for such small-scale accommodation. Doing everything ourselves is not realistic.

118591.explanation: Quality is our top priority, while striving as much as possible for a mix of men and women, including on our candidate list.

118592.explanation: We consider the opinions of residents very important, and in our view, the Council decides too quickly and easily on its own as a 'representative of the people'. A referendum might be a bit much, but we definitely want a clear voice.

118596.explanation: Such initiatives should in principle be supported and encouraged, and we should make maximum use of and be aware of the available (national) subsidies.

118597.explanation: That does not apply in the municipality of Lopik.

118600.explanation: We consider nature and greenery absolutely important. However, a one-to-one replacement is not possible or necessary in every case.

118603.explanation: If residents wish it, we should try to organize it to promote togetherness.

118608.explanation: We find it important that people receiving social assistance can continue to participate (such a scheme/subsidy already exists). A contribution towards this is desirable.

118610.explanation: Some older school buildings certainly need attention; plans are already in place for this.

118611.explanation: We consider stimulation important and could possibly provide support (if financially necessary), similar to membership in a sports club.

118612.explanation: It is important to maintain clubs, also for the livability of the districts. Cooperation and support during difficult times is therefore desirable.

118615.explanation: A feeling of safety is very important to us, and we regularly hear calls for more enforcement officers (boa's). So focusing on this seems like a good idea to us.

118620.explanation: This depends on the circumstances. We should not allow this as a standard matter.

118622.explanation: If possible, that could perhaps be beneficial for all parties.

118626.explanation: Depending on the needs of the residents.

118628.explanation: Professionalism (in healthcare) takes precedence over personal conviction.

118630.explanation: Our municipality’s taxes are already quite high. Other parties too quickly resort to this measure to balance the budget. The residents bear the consequences. This needs and can be done differently.

118631.explanation: Very little is done about it. Especially in rural areas where dogs roam freely on properties, there is only collection of fees without anything in return. However, dog waste is a major annoyance for many residents.

118633.explanation: Expansion of the Copen is highly desirable for our own local businesses.

118634.explanation: Sunday shopping days already exist, and we suspect many residents appreciate this.

118637.explanation: This is undesirable, and there are many other possibilities. It would mar the landscape.

118641.explanation: A fine ambition, but should not be imposed as a strict requirement.

118642.explanation: We find livability in all districts important. However, we will not enforce this, but if districts desire it, Hart voor Lopik is certainly open to encouraging it.

118644.explanation: The municipality is not set up for this. A single project might be feasible, but not too many at once. We must also consider whether this benefits our residents.

118647.explanation: Much is still unclear on this. But if the proceeds benefit the residents, Hart voor Lopik can agree. We also need to look at (European) subsidy opportunities.

141775.explanation: This depends on many circumstances, starting with the opinion of residents, who have not yet been consulted on this. Much remains unclear. Hart voor Lopik is definitely not in favor of housing asylum seekers for neighboring municipalities.

118591.explanation: We aim for diversity in the executive board, but it is not a requirement. Ultimately, quality of the board is what matters to us.

118592.explanation: It's difficult to achieve nuance in a referendum and therefore, in our view, it is not a suitable tool.

118596.explanation: Residents can apply for subsidies from the national government for making their homes more sustainable and generating renewable energy. In addition, residents can get information and assistance with energy savings from Duurzaam Lopikerwaard.

118597.explanation: We want to use our limited industrial space for manufacturing industry, which predominantly exists in our municipality.

118600.explanation: We are a beautiful, green municipality and want to keep it that way.

118603.explanation: The municipality is too spread out to organize a central fireworks show visible to all residents.

118608.explanation: Sports must be accessible even for people with limited financial means.

118610.explanation: The municipality is responsible for building new schools. The school board is responsible for maintaining the buildings.

118611.explanation: The municipality has a youth sports fund and a youth culture fund. Families who cannot afford sports and culture can receive contributions from these funds.

118612.explanation: All sports clubs can apply for subsidies from the municipality. Clubs are responsible for managing their own finances. If necessary, a loan or guarantee can be provided by the municipality.

118615.explanation: There must be a balance between what the municipality can afford and the safety feeling of our residents.

118620.explanation: It is undesirable, as holiday homes are meant for recreation and not for permanent residence. As a municipality, we must ensure more housing so people no longer need to resort to this.

118622.explanation: We should encourage people to do community work in return, but you can't expect this from everyone.

118626.explanation: Charging stations should align with the needs of our districts. If there is a great demand, we must adapt to that need.

118628.explanation: Having good healthcare is more important than choosing between healthcare providers based on personal beliefs. Fortunately, in the Netherlands, people have the right to freely choose their healthcare provider, so they can decide which provider they want to go to.

118630.explanation: On the one hand, costs need to be lowered to relieve residents; on the other hand, we want to guarantee good services.

118633.explanation: We do not want industrial estates in our ribbon developments, but we would like to expand at locations such as De Copen.

118634.explanation: We want to keep it as it is now, with opening hours between 12 p.m. and 5 p.m. that respect church attendance on Sundays. We do not want to extend these hours.

118637.explanation: In principle, we do not want solar panels on land suitable for agriculture. This does not concern areas already designated for sustainable energy generation through solar panels.

118641.explanation: Social rental housing must be appropriate to the location, situation, and needs of the residents. In Lopik, people prefer to buy rather than rent. Therefore, a 30% norm is not always realistic in our view. Besides social rental housing, we also welcome mid-range rental housing.

118642.explanation: It is not appropriate for every village to have a village square (center). However, we do want a community center in every village where people can come together and activities can be organized.

118644.explanation: When opportunities arise to build affordable housing for our own residents, the municipality can support this by purchasing land.

118647.explanation: The municipality must cooperate with residents and entrepreneurs to increase the generation of sustainable energy.

141775.explanation: It is only possible if proper housing is available in our villages for asylum seekers, without disadvantaging our own residents. Otherwise, we prefer shelter at a single location. The number is determined in consultation with the COA and our residents and varies per situation.

118591.explanation: We choose the best people for Lopik. It does not matter to us whether they are men or women. Quality and results count, not fixed quotas.

118592.explanation: Residents must be involved early and have influence. A referendum can help with some decisions, but it is not always the best tool. We choose the most appropriate form of participation per topic.

118596.explanation: Sustainable plans should basically be self-sustaining. Municipal funds are scarce and come from every resident. We prefer to help by reducing regulations, speeding up permits, and supporting savings and insulation efforts.

118597.explanation: No total ban on large companies. They can bring jobs and economic activity. But they must fit within Lopik and not push out our own entrepreneurs.

118600.explanation: We want to abolish the tree-felling permit. You should not need a permit for everything in your own garden. We favor less bureaucracy and more trust. Valuable trees and public greenery will still be protected.

118603.explanation: A municipal fireworks show is expensive, requires a lot of effort, and Lopik is too spread out for one show that pleases everyone. We prefer to spend that money on things that are useful all year round.

118608.explanation: Sports should be accessible, even for those with a small budget. This can be done through existing minimum arrangements and agreements with clubs. We do not want an extra fixed municipal payment. It already works well now.

118610.explanation: Schools must be safe and good, but promises must remain affordable. We follow the current multi-year plan and address what is most needed. Tackling everything at once in such a dispersed municipality is impossible and unrealistic.

118611.explanation: Music is valuable but not something the municipality should pay for every child. We prefer to provide targeted help where needed and give space to associations and offerings in the villages.

118612.explanation: Associations are important but remain responsible for their own finances. We prefer to help with fewer rules and practical support rather than covering deficits.

118615.explanation: This is very important; there are currently too few enforcement officers (boa's) to maintain good supervision in all villages. We want everyone to feel safe in all villages. More enforcement helps against nuisance and makes supervision more visible.

118620.explanation: The housing shortage requires smart choices. If permanent residence occurs anyway, allowing it under clear conditions is better than ignoring it. Safety and livability must be ensured.

118622.explanation: We support a 'reciprocal obligation.' Participation is important. An appropriate reciprocal obligation can help rebuild routine and make the step towards work smaller. However, the human dimension must come first, and the effort should fit the situation.

118626.explanation: We do not want to oppose electric driving but also do not want additional parking pressure. Charging at home should be made easier. Public charging points only where truly needed.

118628.explanation: Everyone deserves good healthcare. We cannot promise that there will always be a provider perfectly matching one's faith or culture. Quality and safety come first; choice is available where possible.

118630.explanation: We want to keep property tax (ozb) as low as possible. To do this, we first make strict spending choices. Unfortunately, we must keep following price developments; otherwise, we risk problems later.

118631.explanation: The dog tax is outdated, unfair, and has no direct connection with facilities for dogs. Therefore, we want to abolish it. This means fewer burdens and less hassle for dog owners.

118633.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be able to grow. That mainly means better use and expansion of existing industrial estates. We will only create new estates if truly necessary.

118634.explanation: People decide for themselves how to spend their Sunday, whether doing nothing or shopping. Entrepreneurs also choose whether to open. Therefore, we do not want a ban on shopping Sundays.

118637.explanation: No total ban but also no unlimited growth. We want clear boundaries so that agricultural land and landscape are protected. Small-scale use for personal consumption should certainly be possible. Large-scale projects will be located around De Copen.

118641.explanation: A fixed percentage of social housing does not work and can delay or even make projects unfeasible. We want to build according to need and ensure a good mix. Starters and middle-income groups must also have opportunities.

118642.explanation: Meeting is important, but not every village has the same scale or needs. Village centers mostly grow historically. Therefore, we do not want an obligation or standardization for every village. Where there is an initiative, we are happy to think along.

118644.explanation: We do not want large risks with land purchases. First accelerate building, utilize what is already available, and cooperate with parties that can deliver. Only make targeted purchases if really necessary or if a unique opportunity arises.

118647.explanation: We do not want an extra money tap for energy projects. Sustainability must be affordable and effective. The focus should be on saving, insulation, and removing rules that block good initiatives.

141775.explanation: We want to fulfill the national obligation as well as possible, but Lopik has limited resources. Shelter is only possible if it is small-scale and manageable, with control over safety and costs. Support and a good implementation weigh more than meeting a paper target. No large-scale projects.

118591.explanation: We strive for equality and inclusivity.

118592.explanation: We find citizen participation very important, but not in the form of a referendum.

118596.explanation: We stimulate citizens who jointly develop initiatives.

118597.explanation: Small-scale development and the character of our landscape must be preserved.

118600.explanation: When trees are felled, at least the same number of trees must be replanted.

118610.explanation: We will continue to support improvements and sustainability.

118611.explanation: Musical development is an important part of our children's growth.

118612.explanation: Sports are for everyone; subsidies for local providers remain important.

118615.explanation: The current capacity of enforcement officers is insufficient.

118620.explanation: Only in temporary and personally related situations.

118622.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits should be positively encouraged based on their personal situation.

118626.explanation: Charging (electric vehicles) must be accessible to everyone.

118628.explanation: Healthcare must be easily accessible, based on trust.

118630.explanation: For both private individuals and businesses, we want to align the property tax (OZB) with surrounding municipalities.

118631.explanation: Hygienic facilities keep our municipality clean.

118633.explanation: We opt to expand De Copen, so that large companies from the rural area can settle there.

118637.explanation: We spare agricultural and natural land and encourage solar panels on (municipal) buildings.

118641.explanation: This is a firm requirement for us.

118642.explanation: Meeting places are important for our communities.

118644.explanation: Managing housing construction requires an active role from the municipality.

118647.explanation: Sustainable entrepreneurship is rewarded. Companies that produce sustainably receive support and, where possible, priority in municipal procurement.

141775.explanation: Our first preference is to distribute asylum seekers proportionally across the communities. This prevents isolation and allows them to participate as much as possible in daily life in our villages. This ensures a human scale.

statements.title: The municipality of Lopik should start purchasing land itself for housing development.
Lopik should deploy more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The residents of Lopik should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Lopik moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Lopik should have more industrial/business parks.
The municipality should spend more money on generating renewable energy.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan het opwekken van

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
At least 30% of new homes built in Lopik should be social rental housing. Currently in the Netherlands 28% of homes are social rentals.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Lopik moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should only accommodate the nationally required number of 93 asylum seekers, distributed across the villages.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet alleen het landelijk verplichte aantal van 93

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.
